Okay, welcome back. In this lesson, we are going to expand on our repertoire, on our list of techniques that we can really use to help our prospect arrive at greater clarity, greater insight, and greater resourcefulness. So this technique is called invitation-based reframing. And this is what it's all about. The best way to resolve the struggle of the past is to transform it into fuel for a better future. Now that might sound like it belongs on a quote card and I apologize for the cheesiness of it, but it really is true, right? Most of the prospects that we will be on the phone with have to a certain degree likely struggled to solve this problem, right? They are full of the remorse around the problems that have arisen. They are full of the memory and trauma and the stress and the upsetness around having had to endure this situation, right? And the best way to resolve all of that baggage that they're bringing to the call is to give them the opportunity to see the light within it, to see where it's brought them, right? And to turn it into fuel for what they really want. However, this only happens once we can view and relate to that struggle, to that challenge, through a lens of greater meaning and purpose. So this is inspired by an email template. I actually taught back in six-figure emails, which you may have access to that one was called markets search for meaning, which in of itself was inspired by Victor Frankel's logo therapy logo therapy markets. Sorry, man search for meaning. Highly recommend that read as well. But here is what is at play. So all hardship, struggle, challenge can ultimately and must be reframed from negative circumstance states into the building blocks for resilience, strength and courage, right? So I'll read that again. All hardship, struggle and challenge can be reframed from a negative circumstance state into the building blocks for resilience, strength and courage. It's the seduction of victimhood and the stories that perpetuate it that keep this natural evolution from occurring, right? All this hardship wishes to be transformed into the building blocks for something better. Everything wants to evolve into something more life affirming, life expanding. However, it's that seduction and lure of victimhood and the stories that perpetuate it, especially what we even validate as coaches that keep that natural evolution from actually happening. And as a conversion coach, you can help facilitate that transformation through inviting your pre client to simply reconsider their experience, not to deny it, not to suppress it, not to sugarcoat it, not to dishonor it, not to minimize it, not to diminish it. None of that, right? We're not trying to bypass the seriousness of the challenges they may have experienced. And at the same time, right, we are inviting them to use it as that fuel, right, to rechannel it towards something greater. And it's vital, like everything else we're talking about, that they arrive at most of this reframe themselves rather than having it told, dictated, lectured or preached to them. Otherwise, it will not be mentally processed as fully as it can. And it certainly won't be embodied and acted on. So what might this look like, you could say, I acknowledge the challenges you've had in keeping your relationship and family afloat as your wife has battled with postpartum depression and volatile mood swings. Is it possible, however, that while things may appear easier outside that container, you would have deprived yourself of the immense growth, strength and resiliency you've experienced over the last year? Or I can understand how hard it must have been having your last launch fail to meet expectations and support your goal of leaving your corporate job this month. But is it also possible to consider that the list you've built along the way, not to mention all the learnings you've extracted around how to launch a program, can be far more valuable in the long run? Or I totally honor that you're not where you want to be with your detoxing goals and you still face these daily challenges in energy and focus. But can we also just take a moment to honor and appreciate how much courage and commitment it's taken to experience even a little bit of improvement over the last year? And how maybe you've set yourself up to have a better chance against this thing now. So, like the compassionate challenge we spoke about in the last lesson, the successful application of this invitation-based reframe should create this crack in the victim story that reinvites power, meaning and resourcefulness to re-enter, right? And ultimately gives them a chance to re-channel all that struggle that they may have experienced, all these negative feelings they may be holding onto and harboring about that situation, giving them a chance to view that through a new lens of a prerequisite or a stepping stone into what they're building towards, which is ultimately the gift that a successful reframe, especially when they were invited to arrive at themselves, gives them. So, that is invitation-based reframing. Take a breather and meet me in the next lesson.
